% mckey-bank.tex -- multiple-choice problems for proving the ANSWER KEY is
% correct, not merely present.  Each problem marks exactly one \cchoice with a
% distinctive needle; the test finds which rendered slot that needle lands in
% and asserts the key's "Answer: X" letter equals that slot (emit_mc_tail's
% letter computation, problem_engine.lua).  Distinct ALL-CAPS needles per option
% so pdftotext can attribute each to a slot unambiguously.

% Plain 4-option MC: correct authored 2nd.  Built WITHOUT \shuffle (mckey-basic),
% so authored order is preserved and the key must read "B".
\begin{problem}{mc_basic}[topic=mc]
	BASICSTEM which option is right.
	\begin{choices}
		\choice BOPTA
		\cchoice BCORRECT
		\choice BOPTC
		\choice BOPTD
	\end{choices}
\end{problem}

% Shuffled: the correct option moves to an engine-chosen slot; the key must
% follow it there.  The test reads the rendered slot of SCORRECT and compares.
\begin{problem}{mc_shuf}[topic=mc]
	SHUFSTEM which option is right.
	\begin{choices}
		\choice SW1
		\cchoice SCORRECT
		\choice SW3
		\choice SW4
		\choice SW5
	\end{choices}
\end{problem}

% [fixed]: authored order must survive \shuffle -> A..D stay FX1,FCORRECT,FX3,FX4
% and the key reads "B".
\begin{problem}{mc_fixed}[topic=mc]
	FIXEDSTEM which option is right.
	\begin{choices}[fixed]
		\choice FX1
		\cchoice FCORRECT
		\choice FX3
		\choice FX4
	\end{choices}
\end{problem}

% \fchoice[3] pins a (wrong) option to slot 3 regardless of the shuffle; the
% correct option floats elsewhere and the key must still name its slot.
\begin{problem}{mc_force}[topic=mc]
	FORCESTEM which option is right.
	\begin{choices}
		\choice GW1
		\cchoice GCORRECT
		\fchoice[3] GFORCED
		\choice GW4
		\choice GW5
	\end{choices}
\end{problem}

% [choose=3] must present exactly 3 options and always include the correct one.
\begin{problem}{mc_choose}[topic=mc]
	CHOOSESTEM which option is right.
	\begin{choices}[choose=3]
		\choice HW1
		\cchoice HCORRECT
		\choice HW3
		\choice HW4
		\choice HW5
	\end{choices}
\end{problem}
